key_insight: Latent electrostatics can be distilled from foundation ML interatomic potentials into a lightweight LES-augmented student model that predicts charges, Born effective charges, and infrared spectra.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster atomistic simulations with explicit electrical response for systems where long-range electrostatics matter, such as liquids, interfaces, and ionic materials.
method: Use teacher-model energies and forces to train a student MLIP with Latent Ewald Summation (LES).
Optionally fine-tune the distilled model with additional DFT data for improved accuracy.
Extract latent atomic charges to compute long-range electrostatics, Born effective charge tensors, and IR spectra.
evidence_signal: The approach was benchmarked on student models distilled from UMA, MACE, Orb, eSEN, GemNet-OC, PET, and EquiformerV2-based teachers against experimental infrared spectra for liquid water, concentrated hydrochloric acid, and the anatase TiO2(101)-water interface.
watch_item: Electrostatic and spectroscopic accuracy depend strongly on the teacher’s underlying DFT level and training data, so distillation cannot fully recover missing physical fidelity.
paper_title: Distilling latent electrostatics from foundation machine learning interatomic potentials
